description.value:
A curved bone-shard the length of a finger, black as obsidian and still warm. It pulses faintly with a sickly golden light. It is warm. And it is hungry.

A piece of Dreyfus's true form — a shape-walker's talon, still resonating with Wyrd-power. This is a dark item: not evil, but it carries the resonance of something that wanted to harm the King. The talon pulses gently, in rhythm with a heartbeat that isn't anyone's in the room.

Acquisition: Only recoverable if the party lands a significant blow on Dreyfus during the Beat 9 confrontation. If he escapes without being wounded, the talon is not recovered.

To Use (Action — One-time Consumable): Throw the talon at a creature within 5 feet. Make an attack roll using Might or Agility (your choice) against the target's Saving Throws. On a hit, the target must make a Hard Presence test (TN 14) or become frightened of the thrower for 1 minute (save ends TN 14). The talon disintegrates after use, crumbling to ash and bone-dust.

Dark Item Note: Kaelen will recognise what this is immediately. If a party member carries it openly, she becomes quietly cold. If they discard it, she relaxes. No religion condemns carrying it, but Ember-Keepers would consider it a violation to keep long-term.

DM Notes

Found: Beat 9 (Shape-Walker's Move) — after Dreyfus escapes, if the party dealt damage to him before the escape. A single talon clatters to the stone floor of the King's chambers, torn free during the violent transformation.

Kaelen's reaction (quiet, cold, looking away) signals she knows what this is. Use her response as social pressure. Groups that keep and use it against a Pale Maw operative gain narrative satisfaction. Groups that destroy it show character. Both are valid.

Beat 24 Connection: If the party carries or used the talon when they face Dreyfus again, note it. His reaction to seeing his own talon is visceral and uncontrolled — the one moment the mask slips.
